---
title: "Publications"
description: "Selected publications by Miguel Cosenza Contreras."
---

Selected publications are listed first with a short note on their role in Miguel's research profile. DOI links are included where known and verified.

## Selected Publications

::: {.publication-card}
### TermineR: Extracting information on endogenous proteolytic processing from shotgun proteomics data
**Cosenza-Contreras M. et al. 2024.** *Proteomics* 24(19). [DOI: 10.1002/pmic.202300491](https://doi.org/10.1002/pmic.202300491)

Software and method paper connected to the [TermineR](https://github.com/MiguelCos/TermineR) R package. It reflects Miguel's work on turning shotgun proteomics data into interpretable information on endogenous proteolytic processing.
:::

::: {.publication-card}
### Proteometabolomics of initial and recurrent glioblastoma highlights an increased immune cell signature with altered lipid metabolism
**Cosenza-Contreras M. et al. 2024.** *Neuro-Oncology* 26(3):488-502. [DOI: 10.1093/neuonc/noad208](https://doi.org/10.1093/neuonc/noad208)

Clinical proteomics and multi-omics integration study combining proteomics, lipidomics, proteogenomics, and clinical interpretation in recurrent glioblastoma.
:::

::: {.publication-card}
### The Schistosomiasis SpleenOME: Unveiling the Proteomic Landscape of Splenomegaly Using Label-Free Mass Spectrometry
**Cosenza-Contreras M. et al. 2018/2019.** *Frontiers in Immunology* 9:3137. [DOI: 10.3389/fimmu.2018.03137](https://doi.org/10.3389/fimmu.2018.03137)

Host-parasite interaction and label-free proteomics study from Miguel's earlier work in biological sciences and proteomics.
:::

::: {.publication-card}
### Proteome alterations in human autopsy tissues in relation to time after death
**Kocsmar E., Schmid M., Cosenza-Contreras M. et al. 2023.** *Cellular and Molecular Life Sciences* 80:117. [DOI: 10.1007/s00018-023-04754-3](https://doi.org/10.1007/s00018-023-04754-3)

Tissue proteomics study relevant to degradation, translational sample quality, and interpretation of post-mortem tissue proteomes.
:::

## Profiles

- [ORCID](https://orcid.org/0000-0002-9727-3229)
- [Google Scholar](https://scholar.google.com/citations?user=DbsUEGsAAAAJ&hl)

## Expanded List

The selected list above is intentionally compact. Future updates can expand this page using ORCID, Google Scholar, PubMed, Crossref, or manually curated BibTeX/CSL metadata.
